name: Niagara Falls
description: One of the world's most famous natural wonders, Niagara Falls offers breathtaking views, boat tours, and a vibrant tourist district with restaurants, casinos, and attractions.
shortDescription: Iconic waterfalls with stunning views and endless attractions.

name: Algonquin Provincial Park
description: A vast wilderness of forests, lakes, and rivers. Perfect for canoeing, hiking, and wildlife spotting. The park is home to moose, bears, wolves, and over 250 bird species.
shortDescription: Vast wilderness park with canoeing, hiking, and wildlife.

name: Wasaga Beach
description: The world's longest freshwater beach stretching 14 km along Georgian Bay. A popular summer destination with warm shallow waters, beach volleyball, and nearby trails.
shortDescription: World's longest freshwater beach on Georgian Bay.

name: Blue Mountain Resort
description: Ontario's premier four-season resort. Skiing and snowboarding in winter, hiking, mountain biking, and the scenic caves in summer. The Village has shops, dining, and activities.
shortDescription: Four-season mountain resort with skiing, hiking, and village life.

name: Tobermory
description: A charming harbour village at the tip of the Bruce Peninsula. Famous for crystal-clear waters, Flowerpot Island, Fathom Five National Marine Park, and the Bruce Trail terminus.
shortDescription: Crystal-clear waters, Flowerpot Island, and the Bruce Trail.

name: Prince Edward County
description: A picturesque island county known for its wineries, artisan food scene, Sandbanks Provincial Park, and charming small towns. A perfect romantic or culinary getaway.
shortDescription: Wine country with beaches and artisan food scene.

name: Muskoka
description: Ontario's cottage country with pristine lakes, charming towns like Gravenhurst and Bracebridge, luxury resorts, and endless water activities. A summer tradition for Torontonians.
shortDescription: Cottage country with pristine lakes and charming towns.

name: Sandbanks Provincial Park
description: Home to the world's largest baymouth barrier dune formation. Three stunning sandy beaches with warm, shallow water make it ideal for families and sunbathers.
shortDescription: Massive sand dunes and warm shallow beaches.

name: Niagara-on-the-Lake
description: A charming heritage town famous for the Shaw Festival theatre, world-class wineries, cycling along the Niagara Parkway, and beautifully preserved 19th-century architecture.
shortDescription: Heritage town with theatre, wineries, and cycling.

name: Elora Gorge
description: A dramatic limestone gorge carved by the Grand River. Popular for tubing in summer, hiking the gorge trails, and exploring the artsy village of Elora with its boutiques and cafes.
shortDescription: Limestone gorge with tubing, hiking, and artsy village.

name: Rattlesnake Point
description: Part of the Niagara Escarpment with dramatic cliff-edge views over the lowlands. Popular for rock climbing, hiking the Bruce Trail, and birdwatching.
shortDescription: Cliff-edge views and rock climbing on the Niagara Escarpment.

name: Scarborough Bluffs
description: Towering 65-metre high cliffs stretching along Lake Ontario's shore within Toronto. Bluffers Park below offers a beach and marina with spectacular views of the bluffs.
shortDescription: Towering lakeside cliffs with a park and beach below.

name: Rouge National Urban Park
description: Canada's first national urban park, spanning from Lake Ontario to the Oak Ridges Moraine. Features hiking trails, wetlands, Carolinian forests, farms, and Indigenous history.
shortDescription: Canada's first national urban park with diverse ecosystems.

name: Toronto Islands
description: A chain of small islands in Toronto Harbour offering car-free beaches, bike paths, picnic areas, an amusement park, and stunning skyline views. A quick ferry ride from downtown.
shortDescription: Car-free island escape with beaches and skyline views.

name: Cheltenham Badlands
description: Unique exposed red Queenston Shale formations resembling a desert landscape. A short boardwalk trail offers views of this geological oddity, formed by erosion of ancient soils.
shortDescription: Mars-like red shale formations — a geological oddity.

name: Dundas Peak
description: A stunning lookout over Dundas Valley and Spencer Gorge. The trail also leads to Tew's Falls, Ontario's tallest waterfall at 41 metres. Part of the Bruce Trail.
shortDescription: Panoramic valley lookout and Ontario's tallest waterfall.

name: Crawford Lake Conservation Area
description: A rare meromictic lake with crystal-clear turquoise water and a reconstructed 15th-century Iroquoian village. Boardwalk trails circle the lake through lush forest.
shortDescription: Rare turquoise lake with an Iroquoian village reconstruction.

name: Sainte-Marie among the Hurons
description: Ontario's first European settlement, reconstructed as a living history museum. Experience 17th-century life through costumed interpreters, traditional crafts, and historical exhibits.
shortDescription: Living history museum of Ontario's first European settlement.

name: Killbear Provincial Park
description: A Georgian Bay gem with windswept pines, rocky shorelines, and sandy beaches. Excellent camping, kayaking, and swimming in clear bay waters.
shortDescription: Georgian Bay park with rocky shores and sandy beaches.

name: Canada's Wonderland
description: Canada's premier amusement park with over 200 attractions including 17 roller coasters, a 20-acre water park, live entertainment, and themed areas for all ages.
shortDescription: Canada's biggest amusement park with 17 roller coasters.

name: Bon Echo Provincial Park
description: Dominated by the massive Mazinaw Rock cliff rising 100m from the lake. Over 260 Indigenous pictographs adorn the rock face. Great camping, canoeing, and cliff-top hiking.
shortDescription: Massive cliff with Indigenous pictographs and pristine lake.

name: Stratford
description: A cultural gem known for the Stratford Festival — one of North America's largest classical repertory theatre events. Also features lovely riverside walks, restaurants, and shops.
shortDescription: Theatre capital with the renowned Stratford Festival.

name: Forks of the Credit Provincial Park
description: A beautiful stretch of the Niagara Escarpment where the Credit River winds through a narrow gorge. Popular for hiking, fall colours, and the iconic Cataract Falls.
shortDescription: Scenic gorge hikes and stunning fall colours.

name: Awenda Provincial Park
description: A quiet park on the shores of Georgian Bay featuring old-growth forest, sandy beaches, and well-maintained hiking trails. Far less crowded than other provincial parks.
shortDescription: Quiet Georgian Bay park with old-growth forest and beaches.

name: Thousand Islands
description: An archipelago of nearly 2,000 islands along the St. Lawrence River. Famous for boat cruises, Boldt Castle, kayaking between islands, and picturesque waterfront towns.
shortDescription: 2,000 islands on the St. Lawrence with cruises and castles.
